title: potentilla rubens var. pusilla
intro: Potentilla rubens var. pusilla, also known as dwarf potentilla, is a low-growing, flowering shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. It is a member of the rose family and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and protect against cell damage. The plant's medicinal properties make it useful for treating a range of health issues, including wounds, eczema, and digestive problems, and it has been traditionally used to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Potentilla rubens var. pusilla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of the plant's medicinal benefits.
